items.question_text: Under 2-10-1, in the en route team concept, there are no absolute divisions of _____ regarding position operations within a sector.
items.explanation: Per 2-10-1a, there are no absolute divisions of 'responsibilities' regarding position operations. The team as a whole has responsibility for safe and efficient operation of the sector.

items.question_text: Under 2-10-1, in en route facilities, the position sometimes referred to as "D-Side" or "Manual Controller" is formally designated as the _____.
items.explanation: Per 2-10-1b3, Radar Associate (RA) is the position sometimes referred to as 'D-Side' or 'Manual Controller.' Understanding the formal terminology for common colloquial position names is important.

items.question_text: Under 2-10-2, in terminal radar facilities, the position commonly referred to as "Handoff Controller" or "Radar Data Controller" is formally the _____.
items.explanation: Per 2-10-2b3, the Radar Associate Position (RA) is commonly referred to as 'Handoff Controller' or 'Radar Data Controller.' This is the terminal facility equivalent of the en route Radar Associate.

items.question_text: Under 2-10-3, in tower operations, the Tower Associate Position is commonly referred to as "Local Assist," "Ground Assist," "Local Associate," or "_____."
items.explanation: Per 2-10-3b3, Tower Associate Position may be commonly referred to as 'Local Assist,' 'Ground Assist,' 'Local Associate,' or 'Ground Associate.' These are all informal names for the same formal position.
